title: corchoropsis crenata var. hupehensis
intro: Corchoropsis crenata var. hupehensis is a lesser-known plant species in the family Tiliaceae, commonly referred to as the Hupeh Stinging Nettle, although it is not closely related to the Stinging Nettle (Urtica dioica). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Corchoropsis crenata var. hupehensis are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can also help to soothe and calm irritated skin. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is prepared as an herbal decoction, ointment, or salve, often in combination with other herbs, to facilitate healing and alleviate symptoms.
